title: Divider component for React layouts
description: Horizontal and vertical Divider component for React and Next.js layouts—separate dense lists, forms, and split panes with subtle Tailwind strokes.

og.title: Divider · Zentauri UI
og.description: Horizontal and vertical Divider component for React and Next.js layouts—separate dense lists, forms, and split panes with subtle Tailwind strokes.

twitter.title: Divider · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: Horizontal and vertical Divider component for React and Next.js layouts—separate dense lists, forms, and split panes with subtle Tailwind strokes.

intro: Dividers add low-noise separation between stacked sections, split panes, and list rows. Ideal for React settings pages and Next.js admin shells that need structure without heavy chrome.
useCases: Split form sections without adding new headings.
Separate comments from primary content in feeds.
Add subtle breaks between toolbar clusters.
Balance dense tables with horizontal rules.

faqs.question: Does the Divider component work with Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Import it like any other React component; keep interactive subtrees in client components when you need hooks or browser APIs, and leave static structure in server components where possible.

faqs.question: Can I customize divider with Tailwind CSS?
faqs.answer: Zentauri UI exposes class-friendly variants and slots so you can extend styles with Tailwind utilities without fighting inline styles.

faqs.question: Is this Divider implementation accessible by default?
faqs.answer: Primitives follow sensible defaults, but accessibility depends on how you label controls, manage focus, and wire keyboard handlers in your app. Validate critical flows with keyboard-only use and screen readers.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
Orientation props cover row vs column stacks.

Combine with spacing utilities rather than hard-coded margins inside the primitive.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Prefer compound subcomponents instead of one oversized prop bag. Export a small, documented API for your design system.

Do not use dividers as the only way to convey grouping—pair with headings or labels.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Keyboard order, focus rings, and ARIA attributes should match production usage. Test with your supported browsers and assistive technologies when semantics are non-trivial.

This preview page exposes a single h1 in the hero for a clean outline.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Colocate examples under the App Router, keep server and client boundaries explicit, and avoid pulling interactive overlays into unexpected server layouts.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL so canonical and Open Graph URLs resolve on deploy.
